top_weaknesses.issue: Thesis (system of record → system of action) is directionally right but already a common AI-SaaS framing
top_weaknesses.fix: Add one contrarian twist, e.g. why the action layer captures budget before it captures the database, or one vertical where incumbents are structurally weak

top_weaknesses.issue: Examples name functions (sales, claims, legal) but never zoom into one specific workflow with before/after
top_weaknesses.fix: Replace one of the function lists with a vivid sales-agent or claims-agent scene: 'agent reads the call, drafts the follow-up, opportunity stage updates Tuesday morning' vs the Friday-afternoon CRM update

top_weaknesses.issue: Gartner $5.74T stat feels bolted on — proves enterprise spend exists, not the records→action shift
top_weaknesses.fix: Either drop the stat or replace it with a citation that supports the action-layer thesis specifically

top_weaknesses.issue: H1 'System of Action' lacks search/category modifier
top_weaknesses.fix: Consider 'AI's System of Action: Why the Next Enterprise Layer Sits Above the Record'

top_weaknesses.issue: VC-essay phrasing trickles in ('value line', 'data gravity', 'AI theater', 'where the margin moves')
top_weaknesses.fix: Trim 2-3 of these and replace with operator language

consensus_summary: Cleanest of the batch so far — strong category frame, shareable closer ('practical source of momentum'). One concrete workflow scene and a contrarian twist would push this from solid to memorable.
